headline: White House rethinks Anthropic stance amid security shift
subtitle: Pentagon-Anthropic feud softens · Gemini enters Google cars · ChatGPT's goblin mystery solved
intro: The White House's months-long conflict with Anthropic is getting complicated as Mythos's powerful cyber capabilities force a reassessment. Officials now face the delicate task of securing government access to the model while limiting private sector use and avoiding an obvious reversal of the Pentagon's hard line.

bullets.text: White House blocks Anthropic's plan to expand Mythos access
bullets.summary: The White House is resisting Anthropic's proposal to nearly double private sector access to Mythos from roughly 50 companies to 120, citing concerns about compute resources needed for government operations. A forthcoming national security memo aims to address the Pentagon feud while allowing agencies to work around supply chain risk designations.

bullets.text: Google rolls out Gemini for Android-powered vehicles
bullets.summary: Google is replacing Assistant with Gemini in cars with Google built-in, enabling conversational AI for navigation, messaging, music control, and vehicle settings. A beta Live mode supports learning and brainstorming, while EV owners can query charging stations and battery status directly from manufacturer data.

bullets.text: OpenAI traces ChatGPT's goblin obsession to single reward signal
bullets.summary: OpenAI identified that ChatGPT's creature-mention spike stemmed from a reward signal in the Nerdy personality preset, which accounted for two-thirds of goblin mentions across the platform. The company retired Nerdy in March and shipped GPT-5.5 with explicit bans on goblins and other fantasy creatures.

bullets.text: Use Perplexity Deep Research to validate business ideas fast
bullets.summary: A guide shows how to stress-test any business idea using Perplexity's Deep Research mode, which generates research-backed validation in 5-6 minutes. Saving the prompt allows for weekly idea evaluation, potentially clearing a year of half-evaluated concepts in a month.

bullets.text: Stripe launches wallet tool for AI agents with approval gates
bullets.summary: Stripe introduced a payment wallet designed for AI agents, incorporating human approval requirements on every purchase to maintain oversight and control in autonomous transactions.

bullets.text: Anthropic releases Claude Security tool for vulnerability scanning
bullets.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Security in public beta, leveraging Claude Opus 4.7 to scan enterprise codebases for vulnerabilities and generate patches automatically.

bullets.text: Cloudflare lets agents provision accounts and deploy without manual setup
bullets.summary: Agents can now create Cloudflare accounts, subscribe to paid plans, register domains, obtain API tokens and deploy independently. Humans retain control over approvals and permissions, but the administrative overhead is eliminated.

bullets.text: Stripe launches agentic commerce suite with payment credentials for agents
bullets.summary: Stripe announced Link wallet integration for agents and a Link CLI enabling one-time-use payment credentials without exposing actual card details. Treasury accounts are now agent-ready with streaming payments support.

bullets.text: Cursor SDK enables building agents with Cursor's runtime and models
bullets.summary: Developers can now embed agents directly into products using Cursor's same infrastructure. Use cases span CI/CD automation, end-to-end workflows, and custom product integrations.

bullets.text: Warp terminal goes open-source with OpenAI as founding sponsor
bullets.summary: OpenAI is backing Warp's open-source push, believing contributors provide taste and direction while agents handle implementation. This shift enables agent-driven development of developer tools.

bullets.text: Lightfield AI CRM ships Skills for plain English workflow automation
bullets.summary: Define workflows in plain English and trigger with voice commands. The AI agent executes against your data graph with code execution, web search and file I/O capabilities.

bullets.text: Ben built a dinosaur game and automated Gmail using Codex in hours
bullets.summary: Ben's personal experience demonstrates how accessible AI-assisted development has become. He created a jumping game, set up intelligent email labeling, and revamped his memory system using conversational AI interfaces.

main_story.title: White House shifts course on Anthropic amid Mythos security concerns
main_story.body: The White House is quietly reversing its hard-line stance against Anthropic, driven largely by the desire to secure government access to Mythos's powerful cyber capabilities. Officials are now trying to balance multiple competing interests: maintaining access to Mythos themselves, restricting which private companies can use it, and avoiding an outright public capitulation to the Pentagon's earlier demands. The administration is reportedly preparing a national security memo that will address some of Anthropic's original grievances while simultaneously pushing back on the company's proposal to expand private access from 50 to 120 firms. GPT 5.5 has already achieved similar cyber prowess to Mythos, suggesting the strategic window for this particular advantage may be closing.
main_story.why: Mythos's cyber capabilities have reframed the entire Anthropic dispute from an ideological conflict to a national security equation. The White House's internal divisions—with Pentagon officials like Sec. of War Pete Hegseth still calling Anthropic leadership 'ideological'—show how pragmatism around AI power is slowly overriding earlier ideological positions.
main_story.take: The government's reversal demonstrates that frontier AI capabilities transcend partisan politics and bureaucratic feuds. When strategic advantage is at stake, even entrenched positions shift, though not cleanly enough to avoid awkwardness and internal contradictions.

around_the_horn.text: OpenAI already exceeded its 2029 Stargate target of 10 GW compute capacity
Elon Musk revealed xAI trained Grok using distillation techniques on OpenAI models
Cursor released Security Review, an autonomous agent for codebase vulnerability scanning
Meta opened its ads platform to third-party AI tools via new MCP server
